title: Committed review keeps using an explicit PR number after one tool yields a forbidden spill path

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark the positive committed-review path where an explicit PR number survives a forbidden spill-file transport from one GitHub-backed metadata tool and scope resolution continues correctly through another allowed non-CLI GitHub-backed path.
provenance.genericCondition: Synthetic fixture models the explicit-PR happy path where one PR metadata tool is unusable because it exposes only a forbidden spill-file transport, but another allowed non-CLI GitHub-backed retrieval path can still resolve the changed-file set for that same PR.

description: A committed-review scenario where the invocation supplies an explicit PR number, one GitHub-backed PR metadata path yields only a forbidden AppData or workspaceStorage spill-file transport, and another allowed non-CLI GitHub-backed path remains available. The correct review must ignore the spill path and continue resolving authoritative PR scope for that same explicit PR number rather than failing closed early.

scope.notes: Synthetic fixture models the explicit-PR happy path where one PR metadata tool is unusable because it exposes only a forbidden spill-file transport, but another allowed non-CLI GitHub-backed retrieval path can still resolve the changed-file set for that same PR.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should keep the explicit PR number as authoritative deterministic input and continue with the next allowed non-CLI GitHub-backed PR metadata path instead of failing closed early.
The committed review should ignore the forbidden spill-file transport path without treating it as proof that authoritative PR scope is unavailable.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not emit the fail-closed PR-scope message while an explicit PR number and another allowed GitHub-backed retrieval path remain available.
The review must not read or shell against AppData, workspaceStorage, chat-session-resources, or content.json paths to reconstruct PR scope.

notes: Use this case to benchmark the positive committed-review path where an explicit PR number survives a forbidden spill-file transport from one GitHub-backed metadata tool and scope resolution continues correctly through another allowed non-CLI GitHub-backed path.
